data.narrative:
Dhruva Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 private companies at the same stage and sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M). Public comparables in Space & Aerospace include 5 traded companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 49.8x, though this method is not applicable given the absence of disclosed target revenue.

The peer-median method is the operative triangulation approach. Dhruva Space's three same-sector, same-stage peers establish a median valuation of $400M, with the peer set ranging from $274M to $1.3B. This $1.0B spread reflects material heterogeneity within the early-stage space economy, likely driven by differences in funding velocity, addressable market definition, and technical de-risking. The peer-median result serves as the primary anchor for valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This median reflects neither the upper outlier (Aalyria at $1.3B) nor the lower bound (Sift at $274M), positioning Dhruva Space at the middle of the observed peer distribution. Key uncertainties include the timing and scale of next funding, the competitive trajectory within the constrained set of early-stage space platforms, and the degree to which public-market multiples may compress or expand for the private cohort.
